title: SVG sanitizer + Tauri CSP
description: Zero SVG sanitization + Tauri csp=null is an arbitrary-code-execution vector (EXPLOITABLE per security review). sanitizeSvgTree() strips <script>/<foreignObject>/<iframe>/<object>/<embed>, all on* attributes, javascript: and data:text/html hrefs. Called from parseSvgString AND clipboard.ts paste. Tauri CSP set to explicit allowlist.
close_reason: sanitizeSvgTree() strips <script>/<foreignObject>/<iframe>/on*-attributes/javascript:-hrefs; called from parseSvgString AND clipboard paste. Tauri CSP set to default-src 'self' with explicit data:/blob:/wasm exceptions. 11 new tests.

title: [graft/spike 3/3] Read + tokenize content-stream operators via mupdf.js
description: Write scripts/spike/03-parse-opstream.ts. For page 0 of the noheader flyer, walk to its /Contents stream via PDFObject API (doc.getTrailer → Root → Pages → Kids[0] → Contents). Call readRawStream() to get raw bytes. Decode if /Filter is set (FlateDecode via node:zlib). Tokenize operators enough to locate a 'Tj' op whose string operand contains 'Kurzfristige'. Report: (1) is there a ready-made operator iterator on mupdf.js or do we roll our own? (2) what's the decoded stream shape (line-oriented? compact?). Determines blast radius of the in-place-edit path. Write findings to temp/spike-03-findings.md.
close_reason: PASS. Custom tokenizer 120 LOC (table-driven per PDF spec §7.8.2) parsed 16KB source content stream into 4264 tokens in 13ms. Op histogram: 106 TJ (array form, per-char positioning), 106 BT/ET text blocks, 112 q/Q save/restore, 27 BDC (tagged content markers — preserved through graft). Zero Tj (plain); source uses TJ exclusively. Finding specific TEXT requires ToUnicode CMap decoding (CID Identity-H fonts use 2-byte glyph indices, not WinAnsi chars) — tractable, +~100 LOC. Phase 3 total: ~250 LOC for tokenizer + CMap + rewrite. tempspike-03-findings.md + scripts/spike/03-parse-opstream.mjs captured.

title: [graft/spike 2/3] mupdf.js graftPage + append overlay content stream
description: Extend scripts/spike/02-graft-overlay.ts. After graftPage (from spike 1), reach into grafted page's /Resources/Font dict via PDFObject API and locate an existing font reference (name like F0/F1). Build a new content stream 'q BT /F1 12 Tf 50 50 Td (hello spike) Tj ET Q' as a PDFObject, append to page's /Contents array (which may be a single ref or an array). Save. Verify via pdftotext: both source text AND 'hello spike' appear in the extracted text. Render via pdftoppm → eyeball screenshot in temp/spike-02-shot.png. Determines whether new-content-append works via this API surface.
close_reason: PASS. Proved both overlay paths: (a) referencing existing grafted source font — works but limited to source's subset glyphs (e.g. Calibri-regular subset had no capital K/H). (b) Embedding our own font (Carlito) into grafted doc via addSimpleFont, registering in page Resources/Font, referencing via new content stream — FULL TEXT renders in green Carlito at bottom-left of page. Source content byte-perfect. pdftotext extracts both cleanly. This is the production pattern for overlays. Artifacts: scripts/spike/02-graft-overlay.mjs, scripts/spike/02b-embed-new-font.mjs, temp/spike-02b-shot-1.png, temp/spike-02-verdict.md.

title: [graft/spike 1/3] mupdf.js graftPage clone: can we duplicate a page verbatim?
description: Write scripts/spike/01-graft-clone.ts. Load temp/Flyer Swift Vortragscoaching yellow BG bluer Border.pdf via mupdf.js. Create new PDFDocument. Call newGraftMap() + graftObject() to deep-copy page 0, or PDFDocument.graftPage() if exposed. Save result to temp/spike-01-graft.pdf. Verify: (1) pdftotext srcPdf == pdftotext result (text extractable, same chars), (2) pdfinfo page dims match A4, (3) pdfimages -all extracts same image hashes. PASS/FAIL gate. Output writes a markdown verdict to temp/spike-01-verdict.md.
close_reason: PASS. 2.17M pixels rendered at 150 DPI via pdftoppm; zero differ between source and grafted-and-saved output. All 10 embedded font subsets preserved with original 6-letter prefixes (NQVAEI+/VKURMS+/BAAAAA+/CAAAAA+ for Calibri + Playfair Display variants). pdftotext output identical. Page dims identical. File size went 2.6MB→1.6MB (graft drops unused cross-page objects). Artifacts: temp/spike-01-verdict.md, temp/spike-01-graft.pdf, temp/spike-01-diff.png.

title: [EPIC] Graft-based PDF round-trip: byte-perfect source fidelity
description: Replace current PDF → MuPDF → SVG → pdf-lib → PDF pipeline with MuPDF graftPage-based round-trip. Untouched source regions preserved byte-for-byte (including embedded fonts, ligatures, kerning tables, hinting). New/edited text shaped via fontkit using the source font when possible. Goal: pixel-perfect fidelity for PDF-edit use case. Contains sub-beads for 3-hour de-risk spike, implementation, and typography polish. See lessons from today's session (pdf-lib Y-flip, Carlito-fallback workarounds). Closes vectorfeld-sqr by making font subsetting moot for source content.

title: Real-PDF export still has text kerning / per-char positioning issues
description: After vectorfeld-dns (leaf transform + tspan position fixes) the composite-via-playwright export is visually mostly correct — headline at top, body in middle, QR + flag at bottom. BUT user reports 'still messed up but better' on 2026-04-20. Residual: per-character spacing/kerning is wrong. Words run together (e.g. 'Ich entlastedurch mein Lektoral'). Root cause hypothesis: MuPDF emits multi-character runs in tspans with PER-CHARACTER x-arrays like x='100 108 116 124' for spacing control. Our drawText currently honours only the FIRST x value via tspan.getAttribute('x').split(/\s+/)[0], then lets pdf-lib lay out the rest with default Helvetica metrics (which don't match the source font's metrics). Fix: iterate per character in tspan when x has multiple values; for each char, drawText at its own (x, y). Or use pdf-lib's drawText with character-spacing if API supports. Verify visually with composite-via-playwright + headed Chromium screenshot.
close_reason: drawText now iterates per character when tspan x/y is a space-separated list. Real-flyer dogfood via headed Chromium: all body text reads cleanly (entlaste durch, Lektorat bei, Erfahrung all now correctly spaced). 3 new tests, 509→512 green. Commit 1d6dbf9.

title: Real-PDF export bugs: leaf-element transforms + <tspan> positioning
description: vectorfeld-9s9 shipped synthetic-test-only and missed two structural bugs that bite every real PDF import: (1) walk() applied transforms on container <g> elements but ignored them on leaf elements (text/path/rect/...), so MuPDF's flatten step (which puts transform=scale(pt-to-mm) directly on each leaf) was silently dropped — content rendered ~3x too large at wrong positions. (2) drawText only read x/y from the <text> element itself, ignoring positions on <tspan> children — but MuPDF emits text positioning ON THE TSPAN. Result: every imported text collapsed to (0,0) and ended up at the bottom-left of the exported PDF. Fixes: walk() now applies the element's own transform before drawing; drawText iterates tspans, drawing each at its own position. 4 new failing tests in test/roundtrip/svgToPdfRoundtrip.test.ts cover both regressions. Verified visually with composite-via-playwright: composite.pdf now matches the canvas layout. Lesson added to docs/lessons.md.
close_reason: Both bugs fixed: walk() now applies leaf transforms; drawText iterates tspans. 4 new failing-then-green tests in svgToPdfRoundtrip.test.ts. Real-PDF composite-via-playwright now produces a visually-correct composite.pdf (verified in headed Chrome PDF viewer). 509/509 tests green.

title: pdf-lib export crashes on non-WinAnsi characters (e.g. ◊ U+25CA)
description: Discovered during composite-via-playwright end-to-end test 2026-04-20. pdf-lib's StandardFonts.Helvetica uses WinAnsi encoding which cannot represent characters outside Latin-1 + extras. The noheader flyer contains '◊' bullets, causing pdf.save() to throw 'WinAnsi cannot encode "◊" (0x25ca)' — entire Export PDF fails, no file produced. Quick fix: substitute or drop unencodeable chars per element with a console.warn so the rest of the export succeeds. Proper fix (separate bead): embed a Unicode TTF font (DejaVu Sans, Noto, etc.) so we cover all common chars without substitution loss.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Synthetic SVG with non-WinAnsi chars (◊, em-dash, etc.) exports without throwing. (2) Substituted chars logged to console.warn. (3) Export PDF round-trip test still green. (4) Composite-via-playwright produces composite.pdf in temp/.
close_reason: safeEncode wrapper in pdfExport.ts catches WinAnsi encoding failures, drops unencodable chars per char with structured console.warn (codepoint listed). Composite-via-playwright now succeeds: 505/505 tests green incl. 2 new encoding tests; composite.pdf delivered (260KB, 1 page, A4); 48 text items extractable via pdfjs-dist getTextContent including German Üöä; only 8 ◊ bullets dropped from the noheader flyer (filed vectorfeld-jc6 for Unicode TTF embedding to preserve those).

title: PDF export via svg2pdf.js does not preserve imported-PDF fonts (round-trip breaks body text)
description: End-to-end test 2026-04-20: File > Open PDF > noheader.pdf, File > Open PDF as Background Layer > yellow BG.pdf, File > Export PDF produces composite.pdf (~1.2 MB, 1 page, valid PDF). But body text is visibly garbled — glyphs misaligned, characters missing (e.g. 'Kurzfristige Hilfe' renders as 'xzfristlge Hilfe'; 'Lektorat von Vortrag' becomes 'ktorlas Vertrragss'). Root cause: svg2pdf.js converts <text>/<tspan> to PDF native text using its own bundled font metrics (Helvetica/Times fallback), which don't match MuPDF's glyph positioning from the original (LiberationSerif etc.). The PDF thus has correct x/y coordinates but wrong glyph widths. SVG export of the same composite is pixel-perfect because it preserves the <text> elements verbatim; the browser renders them correctly because the browser has the real fonts. For round-trip fidelity we need one of: (a) embed original PDF fonts in the exported PDF (requires font subset extraction from source PDF during import), (b) outline all text on export (lossy, non-editable), (c) swap svg2pdf.js for a different engine that handles font embedding. See also vectorfeld-cd2 (import side) — the two together are the PDF-edit pivot's most critical open issue.
close_reason: Production File > Export PDF now uses pdf-lib engine (src/model/pdfExport.ts) end-to-end. Replaces svg2pdf.js + jsPDF for export. Text content survives PDF round-trip; <g transform> composes through walker matrix; primitives (text, path, rect, line, ellipse, circle, image) all supported. 491/491 tests green incl. 13 new round-trip integration tests with extractPdfTextItems position-aware assertions. Bundle: jspdf still imported by test fixture generators; svg2pdf.js fully unwired. Follow-up beads for: bundle cleanup, font-family preservation beyond Helvetica, rotation/skew on rect/ellipse becoming paths.

title: DocumentState context refactor (Phase 1)
description: 9 module-level pub-sub singletons (selection, activeLayer, artboard, grid, guides, defaultStyle, smartGuides, wireframe, keyboardCapture) hold process-scoped state assuming one document. Opening a second PDF silently corrupts all of them. Refactor into state classes with swappable active pointers. documentState.ts aggregates; setActiveDocument(d) atomically swaps every module's pointer. This is the foundational refactor that unlocks multi-doc (Phase 2 = vectorfeld-ipp).
close_reason: 8 singletons wrapped in state classes with swappable active pointers. documentState.ts aggregates; setActiveDocument(d) atomically swaps every module pointer. EditorContext captures and activates one DocumentState at mount. 9 new isolation tests prove multi-doc state independence. Zero consumer-code churn (wrapper pattern).

title: Move MuPDF WASM to a Web Worker
description: MuPDF (10 MB WASM) runs on the main thread. Multi-page import freezes UI for 1-3 seconds. Move to a dedicated Web Worker (pdfRender.worker.ts); pdfImport posts {pdf: ArrayBuffer, pageIndex} with zero-copy transfer, awaits {svg} or {error} response. Worker reused across imports.
close_reason: New pdfRender.worker.ts; pdfImport.ts posts {pdf: ArrayBuffer, pageIndex} (zero-copy transfer), awaits {svg} or {error} response. Worker reused across imports. vite.config.ts: worker.format='es'. Main thread no longer blocks on WASM load.

title: Propagate <tspan> x-arrays on text translate
description: geometry.ts:computeTranslateAttrs updates text.x/y but ignores the per-character <tspan> x-arrays that MuPDF produces. Moving imported text leaves characters stranded. Fix: computeTranslateAttrsAll() + selectTool snapshots/shifts tspan x/y at drag-start, emits ModifyAttributeCommand per tspan so moves are undoable.
close_reason: computeTranslateAttrsAll added + selectTool now snapshots and shifts tspan x/y arrays. 5 new unit tests for the shift function + live verification on real PDF confirms glyphs move as a coherent unit.

title: Auto-ungroup MuPDF top-level <g> on PDF import
description: MuPDF wraps all PDF content in one <g> with the pt-to-mm scale. Clicking any text line selects the whole page — day-1 functional blocker. Fix: prepend the scale transform to each top-level element directly instead of wrapping them, so each <text>/<image> is a direct layer child and individually selectable.
close_reason: Scale transform prepended to each element's transform attribute instead of wrapping in a group; each text/image/g is now a direct layer child and individually selectable. Live verification confirms click on 'Document with Image' selects just the <text>, not the whole page.

title: Fix <image> silently dropped on SVG re-import
description: fileio.ts:255 drawingTags whitelist missing 'image' — re-imported SVGs drop all PDF-imported raster images. One-line fix + regression test. Day-1 blocker per 00-SYNTHESIS.
close_reason: fileio.ts drawingTags whitelist now includes 'image'; added regression test

title: Research: validate MuPDF text=text mode for PDF import round-trip
description: Compare MuPDF path-outline mode (default) vs text=text mode. text=text produces real <text>/<tspan>/<image> elements rather than outlined vector paths. Verify fonts preserved, images survive, elements individually selectable. See docs/stocktake/06-pdf-roundtrip-experiment.md.
close_reason: text=text mode validated: real <text>+<tspan>, fonts preserved, images survive, 5-14x smaller. Switched pdfImport.ts. See docs/stocktake/06.

title: MuPDF text=text fallback: some fonts come through as outlined paths
description: Tested with temp/Flyer*.pdf. PDF 'yellow BG': 225 paths + only 2 text elements — nearly all text outlined to paths. PDF 'noheader': 119 paths + 106 real text + 106 tspan — text preserved. Same MuPDF version, same text=text mode, different fonts (not yet determined which). Outlined text cannot be edited, which breaks the pivot's core 'fix Word-generated PDF' use case for any PDF with outline-falling fonts. Investigate: (a) conditions for MuPDF's text=text fallback to path, (b) surface a warning to user when a PDF has no preserved text, (c) possible MuPDF flag to coerce text preservation even for subset fonts.
notes: EMPIRICAL FINDING (2026-04-20): pdfjs-dist getTextContent() returns the SAME 15 chars as MuPDF on the yellow-BG flyer. Both engines see only 'swift LinguistiK' — the rest is genuinely outlined paths in the SOURCE PDF (designer outlined fonts pre-delivery, common for print PDFs). Cannot be recovered as text without OCR. Reframed scope: (a) detection of mostly-outlined PDFs + (b) user warning on import + (c) data-mostly-outlined attribute on the layer for UI to render a badge. Hybrid pdfjs-dist text overlay is a SEPARATE bead — useful for Type 3 charproc PDFs but no fixture currently exhibits that case.
close_reason: Reframed per empirical finding (2026-04-20): pdfjs-dist returns same 15 chars as MuPDF on yellow-BG flyer, confirming text was outlined-to-paths AT SOURCE PDF GENERATION (designer outlined fonts pre-delivery). Not recoverable as text by any extraction engine without OCR. Shipped: (a) analyzeImportedSvg heuristic detector in src/model/importAnalysis.ts (path-to-text-char ratio with calibrated thresholds), (b) tagLayerWithImportAnalysis hook in pdfImport.ts that sets data-mostly-outlined / data-text-chars / data-path-count + console.warn, (c) ⚠ badge in LayersPanel.tsx with explanatory tooltip. 503/503 tests green incl. 9 analyzer + 3 wiring tests. Filed vectorfeld-pr9 for the orthogonal Type 3 charproc case (where pdfjs-dist CAN extract text MuPDF doesn't — but no fixture currently exhibits that).

title: MuPDF top-level <g> wrapper regression (reopen of vectorfeld-7yc)
description: Yesterday's vectorfeld-7yc close reason claimed 'each text/image/g is now a direct layer child and individually selectable'. Empirical test today (playwright + temp/Flyer*.pdf) disproves this: after File > Open PDF, Layer 1 has exactly one child — a <g> with transform='scale(0.35277...)' containing 112–218 grandchildren. Clicking any text/image still selects the whole page wrapper. pdfImport.ts:154-165 correctly prepends scale to each child's transform, but still inserts the wrapper <g> intact into the layer. Fix: when parsed.layers[i] is itself a MuPDF content-wrapper <g> (no data-layer-name), flatten one level — insert its children directly into the target layer instead of inserting the wrapper. Day-1 functional blocker per 00-SYNTHESIS.
close_reason: pdfImport.ts:applyParsedSvg was still inserting MuPDF's anonymous top-level <g> as a single-child of the synthetic layer (from parseSvgString's no-data-layer-name fallback), giving 'one wrapper + N grandchildren' instead of 'N direct layer children'. Fix: extracted flattenAndScalePdfLayer(layer, scale) that detects single-<g>-child wrappers (no data-layer-name/id/class) and promotes their children one level up while composing wrapper.transform with the scale prefix; falls through to per-child scale if no wrapper is present. 7 new unit tests. 453 tests green (was 446). Live-verified with temp/Flyer*.pdf via playwright: 112 direct layer children post-import (was 1); clicking 'Kurzfristige Hilfe bei englischen Vorträgen' selects the 148.99x10.76 mm headline (was 210x297 mm full page).

title: removeChild crash on null parent during undo
description: AddElementCommand.undo() crashes with TypeError: Cannot read properties of null (reading 'removeChild') when the element's parent was already removed from the DOM (e.g. layer cleared outside command history). document.ts:70 used a non-null assertion on parentElement that fails at runtime. Fixed by adding null guards in removeElement() and AddElementCommand.undo/execute.
close_reason: Fixed: added null guards in document.ts removeElement() and commands.ts AddElementCommand.undo/execute

title: CommandHistory has no size limit - unbounded memory
description: commands.ts undoStack/redoStack grow without bound. Add MAX_HISTORY_SIZE (200) and trim oldest entries.
close_reason: Closed

title: Eraser + DirectSelect hit test ignores transforms
description: eraserTool.ts:26 and directSelectTool.ts:196 use raw getBBox() without transforms. Use transformedAABB from geometry.ts. Also eyedropperTool has its own broken transformedAABB.
close_reason: Closed

title: Eraser tool restores to wrong layer on undo
description: eraserTool.ts:138-141 - undo always appends to current active layer, not original parent. Capture parentElement+nextSibling at erase time.
close_reason: Closed

title: Atrium 5b: regression pass (composite-via-playwright + vitest)
description: Re-run temp/composite-via-playwright.mjs unchanged after all chrome rewrites land, confirm (open foreground PDF -> open background layer -> Export PDF) still produces the expected composite. Also: npm test -- --run must be 512+ green. Red: any new UI changes that break composite flow. Green: pass.
close_reason: All 4 dogfood gates pass: empty state, draw/select, File menu, real-PDF composite. 600/600 vitest green. One real bug caught (LeftRail subscribe-after-notify) + fixed.

title: Atrium 4: bundle web fonts locally (Inter, JetBrains Mono, Playfair Display)
description: Bundle woff2 for Inter (400/500/600), JetBrains Mono variable, and Playfair Display regular in src/fonts/. Add @font-face declarations to src/index.css. Use Inter for chrome (--font-ui), Playfair for hero text in empty state (--font-serif), JetBrains Mono for pill readouts (--font-mono). No Google Fonts CDN → Tauri CSP stays untouched. Red: getComputedStyle on body returns 'Inter' as first font family after bundle; fonts load via file://. Green: download woff2, wire @font-face.
close_reason: Bundled Inter (400/500/600) + JetBrains Mono 400 via @font-face. No CDN; CSP untouched. 600/600 tests green. Fonts confirmed loaded in headed chrome.

title: Atrium 5a: Atrium dogfood playwright script
description: temp/atrium-dogfood.mjs: headed Chromium, opens localhost:5173, screenshots empty state, opens a real PDF from temp/, screenshots compositing state, asserts panels are absolute-positioned, toolrail has 9 buttons, active tool has accent-tint bg, Inspector populates on text selection. Per the persistent memory rule: this is the acceptance gate before declaring the redesign done. Red: script currently does not exist. Green: write it + run against live dev server + commit screenshots into docs/redesign-atrium/.
close_reason: All 4 dogfood gates pass: empty state, draw/select, File menu, real-PDF composite. 600/600 vitest green. One real bug caught (LeftRail subscribe-after-notify) + fixed.

title: Atrium 3f: Ruler repalette + reposition
description: Ruler.tsx: keep canvas-based rendering but swap hardcoded #f0f0f0 / #ccc / #333 for Atrium tokens (read from getComputedStyle on document.documentElement at render time). 14px thickness per README (currently 20px). Opacity 0.6 per design. Red: rulers render; text color matches --muted token. Green: restyle + thickness change.
close_reason: Ruler repalettted + thinned to 14px with Atrium tokens. 598/598 green. Headed screenshot.

title: Atrium 3g: FillStrokeWidget + ColorPicker + ArtboardDialog + ContextMenu restyle
description: Four small visual restyles, behavior unchanged: (1) FillStrokeWidget swatches: w-6 rounded with --border-strong ring, swap button uses IconGlyph; (2) ColorPicker popover: Panel treatment, preset palette = Atrium preset row from atrium.jsx AtInspector Color section (7 color hexes); (3) ArtboardDialog: Panel treatment, accent-gradient Apply button; (4) ContextMenu: Panel treatment, hover row uses --accent-tint. Red: each renders with the Atrium Panel treatment (data-role='panel'). Green: restyle pass.
close_reason: FillStrokeWidget, ColorPicker, ContextMenu, ArtboardDialog all restyled to Atrium treatment. 598/598 tests green. Headed ArtboardDialog screenshot captured.

title: Atrium 3d: LayersPanel row restyle (pre-merge)
description: Restyle LayersPanel.tsx rows per atrium.jsx AtLayerNode: 30px height, kind-based thumb (folder=icon / rect=accent swatch / image=peach / text='T' serif / group=outlined square), indent by depth, italic when data-font-style='italic', 'mostly outlined' ⚠ badge when data-mostly-outlined attr is present, accent-tint selection. Keep existing data bindings — do NOT rebuild the subscription layer. This will later be placed inside the Inspector card (3e) but ships first as a standalone panel for visual regression screenshots. Red: element-type thumbs render; ⚠ badge appears for layer with data-mostly-outlined='true'. Green: restyle.

Delete the standalone LayersPanel mount in App.tsx. Red: selection header text updates on setSelection; Frame pills edit via ModifyAttributeCommand; switching tabs swaps Layers<->Pages. Green: rewrite.

Middle: page navigator (inert for single-page PDFs; wires up when multi-page lands). Right: zoom -/+/% (wire to existing getZoomPercent / zoomAtPoint). Red: renders saved indicator, zoom %, and zoom buttons invoke zoomAtPoint with correct sign. Green: rewrite.

Tool roster (top-to-bottom): select V, directSelect A, pen P, brush B, text T, rect R, knife K, eyedropper I, erase E. Brush + Knife: show disabled + tooltip 'Coming soon' (design key brush/knife have no backing tool). Active: var(--accent-tint) bg + var(--accent-text). Overflow dot below divider lists hidden tools (pencil, measure, lasso, free-transform) in a dropdown. Red: 9 rail buttons render; brush+knife disabled; active tool gets accent-tint. Green: new component + wire into App.tsx (replaces <ToolStrip/>).

title: Atrium 3b: TopBar component (tabs stub + export)
description: Replace MenuBar.tsx with src/components/TopBar.tsx per atrium.jsx AtTopBar. Floating translucent card, h:44. Contents: brand mark (linear-gradient square), File/Edit/View/Object menu words (reuses existing MenuBar menus prop for dropdown logic but new pill visual treatment), single-doc tab stub showing active filename (pill, dirty dot from CommandHistory.canUndo, close X inert until multi-doc), Split button (inert stub for vectorfeld-4w7), Export PDF accent-gradient button wired to existing exportPdf(). Red: tab shows 'Untitled' when no import; dirty dot appears after a command executes; clicking Export PDF invokes exportPdf. Green: component + App.tsx wire.

title: Atrium 2: layout restructure — floating panels

Replace docked flex + CSS-grid chrome with absolutely-positioned floating panels over a radial-gradient canvas root (position: absolute; inset: 0). Delete propsCollapsed + layersCollapsed state and their toggle UI. Rulers reposition to inside the canvas area (top:66 left:78 per design). Panel positions per README.md handoff: TopBar top:12/left:12/right:12 h:44, LeftRail left:12/top:72 w:58, LayersPanel left:82/top:72/bottom:60 w:240 (will be merged into Inspector in 3e), Inspector right:12/top:72/bottom:60 w:286, StatusBar bottom:12/left:12/right:12 h:40. Red: data-testid=topbar/leftrail/inspector/statusbar are present; canvas root background-image contains 'radial-gradient'. Green: replace JSX.
close_reason: Layout restructured to Atrium floating panels. 7 new tests pass, 570 total green, clean tsc, headed screenshot captured.

title: Atrium 1c: retire old icons.tsx
description: Delete src/components/icons.tsx. Update ToolStrip.tsx (to be replaced in 3a anyway) and any other consumer of TOOL_ICONS to use IconGlyph + iconKeyForTool. Red: tsc -b clean + existing ToolStrip tests still green. Green: sweep consumers.

title: Atrium 1a: IconGlyph + ICONS dictionary
description: Port ICONS (28 glyphs) + IconGlyph + IconLabels from design/unpacked/design_handoff_vectorfeld/icons.jsx verbatim to src/components/IconGlyph.tsx. Hairline default (1.25px stroke, round caps/joins, fill=none). Expose size prop (default 20). Red: render <IconGlyph name='select'/> asserts <svg viewBox='0 0 20 20'> with stroke-width='1.25'; every IconGroups id resolves to a valid ICONS entry. Green: verbatim port.

title: Atrium 1b: iconKeyForTool alias map
description: src/components/iconKeyForTool.ts: translate registered tool names to ICONS keys. Aliases: rectangle->rect, eraser->erase, direct-select->directSelect. All other current tools map 1:1 (select, pen, text, line, ellipse, pencil, measure, lasso, free-transform->select-fallback, eyedropper). Red: for every tool returned by getAllTools(), iconKeyForTool(name) resolves to an ICONS entry. Green: write the map.
close_reason: Alias map ships with 15 passing tests covering all 13 registered tools + passthrough.

title: Atrium 0c: Panel wrapper component
description: src/components/Panel.tsx: tiny wrapper that applies the floating translucent-card treatment (background: var(--panel); backdropFilter: var(--blur); border: 1px solid var(--border); borderRadius: 14; boxShadow: var(--shadow-panel)) to any child. Used by TopBar, LeftRail, Inspector, StatusBar. Red: render <Panel>x</Panel> and assert data-role='panel' + inline style contains backdropFilter. Green: 40-LOC component.
close_reason: Panel wrapper shipped with 5 passing tests covering data-role, CSS-var surface, style/className passthrough.

title: Atrium 0a: oklch theme tokens in index.css
description: Replace --color-chrome-* + --color-accent in src/index.css @theme with the oklch Atrium palette: --bg, --canvas-tint, --panel, --panel-solid, --border, --border-strong, --text, --muted, --faint, --accent, --accent-tint, --accent-text, --blur, --radius-panel, --shadow-panel. Keep --color-artboard. Red: test that getComputedStyle on document.documentElement returns the new values. Green: swap @theme block.
close_reason: Swapped src/index.css @theme to oklch Atrium palette verbatim. 18 new tests pass; 531/531 total green. Commit in tree.

title: Atrium 0b: theme/atrium.ts palette module
description: Export defaultAtrium + birrenSage + birrenPeach + sodiumVapor objects from src/theme/atrium.ts (verbatim from design/atrium.jsx). V1 uses defaultAtrium only; the others exist for a future theme switcher. Red: snapshot test with exact key set. Green: port objects.
close_reason: Ported 4 palettes + Palette type + palettes record to src/theme/atrium.ts. 5 new tests pass.

title: Epic: Replace UI shell with Atrium design
description: Umbrella epic for the full visual/chrome redesign per design/unpacked/design_handoff_vectorfeld/. Replaces docked flex+grid chrome with absolutely-positioned floating translucent panels over a tinted canvas. Oklch warm palette + coral accent, 14px radius, 1.25px hairline icons, 28-glyph ICONS dictionary + IconGlyph renderer. Decisions locked 2026-04-23: (1) brush+knife show disabled w/ tooltip, (2) Properties+Layers merge into one Inspector card with Layers tab at bottom, (3) tab strip renders a single stub tab now, multi-doc wiring waits for vectorfeld-4w7, (4) collapse toggles dropped entirely, (5) bundle web fonts locally (Inter + JetBrains Mono + Playfair Display; Tauri CSP untouched).
close_reason: Atrium UI redesign shipped end to end. 15 sub-beads (0a/0b/0c/1a/1b/1c/2/3a/3b/3c/3d/3e/3f/3g/4/5a/5b) closed via red-green TDD + headed-Chromium dogfood. 600/600 vitest green across 49 files (+37 new Atrium tests). Real-PDF compositing regression intact. Two real bugs caught via dogfood: TopBar overflow clipping, LeftRail subscribe-race.

title: [graft/phase 3] In-place source text edit via grafted source font
description: The pickiest use case: user clicks a source-PDF text element, edits the string. Today would become mask+re-render in our Carlito — visible seam. Correct version: extract the source font bytes from the grafted PDF (FontFile2 stream), hand to fontkit, re-shape the edited string with the SOURCE font. Emit TJ rewriting just that op-range in the source content stream. Result: edited text blends invisibly with untouched surroundings — same font, same kerning style, same ligature behavior. Requires spike 3 content-stream parser + spike 2 content-stream writer. ~300 LOC.

title: [graft/phase 3] fontkit shaping for new (non-source) text
description: When the user creates new text via the text tool (AddElementCommand, not from PDF import), shape via fontkit.layout(string) using our bundled Carlito/Liberation Serif. Emit raw PDF TJ with glyph indices + positioning adjustments rather than pdf-lib drawText (which handles basic cases but not all OpenType features). Result: correct fi/fl ligatures in Carlito, correct GPOS kerning pairs for pairs like Ta/AV/Wa. ~200 LOC + tests for ligature presence in output TJ array.

title: [graft/phase 2] Real-flyer byte-diff round-trip test
description: Extend test/roundtrip: import the noheader flyer via the full import path, export with ZERO edits, compare content-stream bytes of each page against the source. For untouched regions this should be IDENTICAL after graft (modulo PDF xref offsets). Add as a regression gate for the graft engine. If bytes diverge, the test should print which operator ranges differ so future regressions are easy to track down. Also: headed-Chromium screenshot diff via pixelmatch as a cross-check.

title: [graft/phase 2] Wire graftExport into fileio.ts exportPdf
description: Swap the svgStringToPdfBytes call in exportPdf when doc has sourcePdfHandles. Keep legacy pdf-lib walk() path as fallback for pure-SVG documents (no PDF import involved). Preserve existing download flow. Update exportSvgStringToPdfBytes test helper to accept an optional sourcePdfHandles so tests can drive the new path. ~30 LOC.

title: [graft/phase 2] New engine: src/model/graftExport.ts
description: New export engine. Entry: exportViaGraft(doc: DocumentModel, sourcePdfHandles: Map<layerId, mupdfHandle>) → Uint8Array. Strategy: (1) Create new mupdf PDFDocument. (2) For each layer in z-order: if layer has a sourcePdfHandle AND no element in it has touchesSource=true → graftPage that layer's page into the new doc. If layer is overlay-only (SVG-authored or edits) → render via existing pdf-lib drawing path (now only used for new content). (3) For each source-touching element: Phase-2 compromise = mask with white rectangle + re-render the edited element on top via pdf-lib (preserves everything around it; the edited element uses our font stack). Phase 3 upgrades this to content-stream rewrite. (4) Save. ~400 LOC.

title: [graft/phase 2] Command-level overlay vs source-touch classification
description: Extend Command interface in src/model/commands.ts with touchesSource(): boolean. Default false (pure-overlay). Override in: ModifyAttributeCommand when target element has data-src-page attr → true. RemoveElementCommand on tagged → true. CompoundCommand → OR of children. Used by export path to decide: graft-through vs mask+re-render vs content-stream-rewrite. No behavior change yet — this bead just adds the metadata. ~40 LOC + 3 test cases.

title: [graft/phase 2] Tag SVG elements with source-PDF back-references
description: During PDF→SVG extraction (src/model/pdfRender.ts runs in worker), tag each emitted <text>, <path>, <image> with data-src-page (int), data-src-layer-id (string, the layer name), and for text with data-src-op-offset (byte offset into the source page's content stream where the originating Tj/TJ op starts). The op-offset is the bridge for in-place text edits later. Requires spike 3 findings to know whether we get offsets from mupdf.js or need a custom tokenizer. ~50 LOC + dependent on spike-3.

title: [graft/phase 2] Live mupdf.js Document handle in DocumentState
description: Currently src/model/pdfImport.ts disposes the mupdf.js Document immediately after SVG extraction (in the worker + main-thread cleanup). Change: keep the PDFDocument alive at DocumentState level — per-document. Add { sourcePdf?: { bytes: Uint8Array; pageCount: number; mupdfHandle: unknown } } to DocumentState. For background-layer imports, keep parallel mupdf handles keyed by layer name. Tie lifecycle to document close. Impact: pdfImport.ts, pdfImport.backgroundLayer, documentState.ts, pdfRender.worker.ts message protocol. ~150 LOC.

title: Embed Unicode TTF font in pdf-lib export to preserve non-WinAnsi chars
description: Per vectorfeld-ape, the pdf-lib export currently uses StandardFonts.Helvetica which has WinAnsi encoding. Characters outside Latin-1 + extras (e.g. ◊ U+25CA used as bullets in Word PDFs, em-dashes, fancy quotes, CJK, math symbols) get DROPPED during export with a console.warn. Fix: bundle a permissively-licensed Unicode TTF (DejaVu Sans / Noto Sans) and pdf.embedFont(ttfBytes, { subset: true }) so the export preserves arbitrary text. Bundle hit ~80-200KB (subset to chars actually used). Acceptance: composite-via-playwright produces composite.pdf with ◊ bullets visible.
close_reason: Embedded Carlito (Calibri clone) + Liberation Serif (Playfair Display substitute) via @pdf-lib/fontkit. Non-WinAnsi chars like ◊ now survive Export PDF. Real-flyer composite visually matches source fonts at the glyph level. Commit a8d2fe6.

title: PDF round-trip golden-fixture test harness
description: Build a vitest-based test harness that asserts PDF round-trip fidelity (open PDF → edit → export → re-open). Required infrastructure for TDD work on vectorfeld-9s9 and vectorfeld-cd2 — both P1 bugs need a failing-test scaffold to drive their fixes. Stack: pdfjs-dist + node-canvas for raster rendering, pixelmatch + pngjs for diff, plus an SVG normalizer (strip ids, round coords to 2dp, sort attrs) for the high-signal semantic diff. pdfjs-dist needs the node env so per-file '// @vitest-environment node' directive. Phase A (this bead): scaffold + unit-tested helpers + small refactor to expose pdfBytesToSvg pure helper from pdfImport.ts. All committed code stays GREEN. Phase B (vectorfeld-9s9): write failing integration test + fix svg2pdf engine swap + commit together (red→green TDD cycle).
acceptance_criteria: (1) test/roundtrip/{fixtures,golden,helpers}/ scaffold exists. (2) helpers/normalizeSvg.ts with unit tests. (3) helpers/renderPdf.ts with unit tests producing deterministic bytes. (4) pdfBytesToSvg exported from pdfImport.ts. (5) npm test -- --run all green. (6) AGENTS.md updated with one-liner pointing to test/roundtrip/.
close_reason: Phase A landed: scaffold test/roundtrip/{fixtures,golden,helpers}/, normalizeSvg helper (10 tests), renderPdfPageToPng helper (4 tests), extracted pure renderPdfPageToSvg from worker into src/model/pdfRender.ts, pdfPipeline composer (3 tests). 478/478 green, tsc clean. AGENTS.md pointer added. Unblocks vectorfeld-9s9 + cd2 for TDD red-green.

title: PDF export: yellow-BG composite has left-edge white margin + possible content clipping
description: Observed in composite.pdf (output of end-to-end test). The yellow background rectangle appears truncated on the left — a ~15-20mm white strip is visible at the left edge, and some content near the page edges may be clipped. SVG export of the same composite renders correctly (yellow fills full page). Likely cause: svg2pdf.js handles viewBox/transform composition differently than browser, or clipPath elements (112 clipPaths in this PDF's defs) don't translate cleanly. Investigate: (1) does svg2pdf.js honor the inherited scale transform from flattenAndScalePdfLayer, (2) does it process <defs> clipPath correctly, (3) is the issue specific to this PDF or reproducible with simpler content. Lower priority than font fidelity but part of the same round-trip-fidelity-via-svg2pdf.js story.

title: Multi-document UI + cross-document clipboard (DocumentState Phase 2)
description: The defining feature of the PDF-edit pivot. Phase 1 of the DocumentState refactor landed; this builds the UI on top of it. Concrete sketch: (1) EditorProvider holds a mutable list { id, model: DocumentModel, history: CommandHistory, state: DocumentState }[] instead of one of each. (2) A tab strip UI above the canvas (or under the menu bar) lists open docs with close-buttons. Clicking a tab calls setActiveDocument(state) and swaps docRef.current, historyRef.current accordingly. (3) File -> Open PDF creates a new document entry and switches to it (instead of replacing the current doc). (4) Clipboard ref lifted from EditorProvider to a module-level or app-level location so it's shared across all open docs; copySelection/pasteClipboard read from the shared clipboard and operate on the active doc's model/history. (5) Keyboard shortcuts Ctrl+Tab, Ctrl+1..9 — nice-to-have.

title: Rewrite AGENTS.md + API.md to match reality post-pivot
description: Docs are load-bearing wrong. AGENTS.md claims Phase 2 complete / 472 tests / zero errors — reality is different. API.md documents a deleted Toolbar.tsx, 5 tools when there are 13, polling-based LayersPanel that was replaced by subscriptions. New agent sessions build wrong mental models. Rewrite both.
close_reason: AGENTS.md rewritten from scratch: concise pivot statement + current-state snapshot + honest 'what's not here' list + workflow commands. API.md surgical rewrites: orientation block, File I/O section (adds exportPdf/exportPng/placeImage/importPdf/sanitizeSvgTree), Tools section (5→13 with visibility column), keybindings (5→13), Components (deletes Toolbar, adds ToolStrip/MenuBar/ControlBar/FillStrokeWidget/Ruler/ContextMenu).

title: Hide authoring-only tools from ToolStrip
description: Cold-pickup shows 13 tools, most useless for PDF editing (reviewer scored UX 3/10). Expand HIDDEN_TOOLS to hide pen, pencil, measure, lasso, free-transform, eyedropper. Keyboard shortcuts still reach hidden tools for power use.
close_reason: HIDDEN_TOOLS expanded to hide 6 pure-authoring tools (pen/pencil/measure/lasso/free-transform/eyedropper); cold-pickup now sees 7 tools (select, line, rectangle, ellipse, eraser, text, direct-select). Keyboard shortcuts still work for hidden tools.

title: Delete old-PRD dead code (post-pivot cleanup)
description: Remove modules that served the abandoned scientific-diagram PRD and have no role in the PDF-edit pivot: tikzExport, offsetPath, textPath, compoundPath, pathBooleans, clipping, opacityMask, swatches, SwatchPanel, scissors tool, knife tool, matching Object-menu items, Paper.js dep. Target: ~2000 LOC + ~359 KB Paper chunk eliminated.
close_reason: Deleted 2126 LOC: tikzExport, offsetPath, textPath, compoundPath, pathBooleans, clipping, opacityMask model modules + tests; SwatchPanel + swatches; scissors+knife tools + icons; matching App.tsx imports and ~190 lines of Object menu items. Removed paper dep. 437 tests green (was 489, deleted tests removed). Main bundle ~20KB smaller + paper chunk 359KB eliminated.

title: Pre-split smart-guide candidates by axis
description: smartGuides.cacheSmartGuideCandidates filters candidates by axis every RAF frame during drag. Pre-split into {x[], y[]} at drag-start so computeSmartGuides is a pure lookup. Performance cliff at real-document scale.
close_reason: cachedCandidates now stores pre-split {x[], y[]} instead of a mixed list with per-frame filter; computeSmartGuides is pure lookup. 10 tests still green.

title: Paste in Place (Ctrl+Shift+V) — no duplicate offset
description: Today's Ctrl+V paste adds 5 mm x/y offset (standard Illustrator duplicate behavior) — the <g> lands at matrix(..., 5, 5) instead of matrix(..., 0, 0). For compositing, exact overlay is wanted. Add Paste in Place command bound to Ctrl+Shift+V that pastes clipboard at original coordinates.

title: File > Open PDF as Background Layer (compositing primitive)
description: Single-doc model + replace-on-open makes the composite-PDF use case require: open foreground, Ctrl+A, Ctrl+C, open background (destroys canvas), Ctrl+V, Ctrl+Shift+[. Verified working end-to-end today with temp/Flyer*.pdf. Users would not discover this workflow. Proposal: add second File menu item 'Open PDF as Background Layer…' that (1) creates a new layer named after filename, (2) imports the PDF into it, (3) sends the new layer to bottom of layer stack, (4) does not touch existing layers. Covers 80% of compositing without waiting for vectorfeld-4w7 multi-doc tabs.
close_reason: Added File > Open PDF as Background Layer... menu item. Imports a PDF as a new <g data-layer-name> positioned BEFORE existing layers (bottom of z-stack = rendered first = behind). Layer name = filename minus '.pdf' extension, truncated to 40 chars with ellipsis; empty/whitespace stems fall back to 'Background'. Reuses flattenAndScalePdfLayer so new layer has direct children (not wrappers). Does not touch viewBox or clear existing layers; defs are appended. Bug found and fixed during verification: clearSelection() was being called BEFORE the layer insertion, so LayersPanel's selection subscription saw the old state; moved to end of function. Live-verified against temp/Flyer*.pdf — 3-click composite (Open PDF noheader.pdf → Open PDF as Background Layer yellow BG.pdf → done). Both layers show in Layers panel with correct names and z-order. 8 new unit tests: insertion position, existing-layer preservation, viewBox not touched, pt→mm scale applied, filename sanitization (strip extension, truncate, Background fallback). 461 tests green.

title: Add Arrange submenu to Object menu (Bring to Front / Send to Back)
description: Object menu today shows only Flip H/V, Convert to Path, Join Paths. Z-order operations exist in src/model/zOrder.ts (bringForward, sendBackward, bringToFront, sendToBack) and are bound to Ctrl+[/], Ctrl+Shift+{/} in EditorContext.tsx. They are undiscoverable via menu — required keyboard knowledge to complete the composite-PDF workflow. Add Arrange submenu with 4 items wired to existing functions. Reference PRD Sprint 7 #61 (arrange z-order was in original MVP).
close_reason: Added 4 z-order items to Object menu between Flip (transforms group) and Convert to Path (path ops group), separated by dividers: Bring to Front (Ctrl+Shift+]), Bring Forward (Ctrl+]), Send Backward (Ctrl+[), Send to Back (Ctrl+Shift+[). Wired to existing zOrder.ts functions; keyboard shortcuts already worked, now also discoverable via menu. Fixed MenuBar wrap by adding whitespace-nowrap so long labels don't line-break. Live-verified: menu opens, shows 3 logical groups, shortcuts aligned right, items correctly disabled when no selection. All 453 tests green.

title: No UI for rx/ry (rounded corners) on rect elements
description: The Properties panel has no input fields for rx and ry attributes on rect elements. Rounded rectangles from imported SVGs display correctly, but there is no way to create or modify rounded corners through the UI. This was found via benchmark comparison with W3C shapes-rect-01.svg which has rects with rx=30 and ry=50.

title: Default style bleeds fill to subsequent drawings
description: When drawing multiple rects in sequence with different fill modes (solid then none), the default style system leaks the previous fill color to the next drawing. Discovered via benchmark redraw: after setting R2 fill=fuchsia then drawing R3 with setFillNone(), R3 still gets a black fill instead of none. The defaultStyle.ts module doesn't properly reset when switching between fill types via the Properties panel.

title: Stroke color picker has allowNone=false - cannot set stroke=none via Properties panel
description: The ColorPicker for stroke in PropertiesPanel.tsx (line 478) passes allowNone={false}, which means the 'None (transparent)' button is not shown. Users cannot remove stroke via the Properties panel color picker. The only workaround is setting stroke-width to 0, which is semantically different from stroke=none. The fill color pickers for gradients (lines 538, 558) also have allowNone={false}.

title: Pen tool cannot create asymmetric bezier control handles
description: Click-drag on the pen tool creates symmetric incoming/outgoing handles at each anchor point. SVG paths frequently have asymmetric control points (different lengths/angles for incoming vs outgoing handles). Benchmark test showed 44mm error on a control point where the reference had asymmetric handles. Need a modifier key (e.g., Alt+drag) to break handle symmetry and adjust one side independently.

title: Pen tool cannot produce S/s (smooth curveto) path commands
description: The pen tool only generates M, L, C, Z commands. It cannot produce S (smooth curveto) or s (relative smooth curveto), which reflect the previous control point automatically. SVG benchmark paths-data-01 requires S/s for X-curve, Ribbon, and Circle paths. The pen tool would need a mode where clicking an anchor auto-reflects the incoming control handle to create a smooth continuation.

title: Drawing tools don't respect layer lock
description: rectTool, ellipseTool, lineTool, penTool, pencilTool all call getActiveLayer() without checking data-locked='true'. Elements can be created on locked layers. Add lock check after getActiveLayer() in each tool's commit handler.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add RAF throttling to mouse move handlers
description: Canvas.tsx:249 and selectTool mouse move fire on every native event without requestAnimationFrame batching. Rulers also redraw fully on every cursor move.
close_reason: Closed

title: Knife tool multi-intersection splitting uses wrong segment indices
description: knifeTool.ts:116-128 - after first split, remaining hits still reference segment indices from original path. Need to adjust indices relative to remaining piece.
close_reason: Closed

title: freeTransformTool rotate doesn't preserve base angle
description: freeTransformTool.ts:182-193 - rotation only uses mouse delta, not baseAngle+delta. Existing rotation is overwritten.
close_reason: Closed

title: Selection overlay rebuilds all DOM elements every frame
description: selection.ts:139-259 - updateOverlay removes+recreates all children on every call including during drag. Cache elements, only update positions.
close_reason: Closed

title: PDF import appends layers after overlay groups
description: pdfImport.ts:141-143 - imported layers render on top of selection handles/grid/guides. Use insertBefore.
close_reason: Closed

title: Extract shared hitTest to eliminate 4-way duplication
description: selectTool, eraserTool, eyedropperTool, directSelectTool each have their own hitTest with subtle differences. Extract to model/hitTest.ts.
close_reason: Closed

title: [graft/cleanup] Update AGENTS.md + docs/API.md for graft architecture
description: Document the new model. Update API.md: new graftExport.ts surface; live mupdf handle in DocumentState; data-src-* element tagging. Retire obsolete lessons from docs/lessons.md that assumed SVG round-trip as primary (e.g. synthetic-test-blindspot for MuPDF emission shape — still valid for SVG authoring path, but no longer applies to PDF-imported content). Update AGENTS.md 'current state' section. ~1h.

title: [graft/cleanup] Retire pdfExport walk() rendering for PDF-source content
description: Once graftExport is in production and the round-trip byte-diff test is green: delete the source-rendering paths in src/model/pdfExport.ts — drawPath/drawRect/drawLine/drawEllipse/drawCircle/drawImage/drawText. Keep only what pure-SVG export needs (still useful if user authors from scratch). Subset Carlito/Liberation (closes vectorfeld-sqr) now that they're only used for new user-authored text, not full-doc rendering. Likely -300 LOC + -1MB bundle.

title: Subset fonts on export to shrink PDF size
description: Current Export PDF embeds full TTFs (Carlito + Liberation Serif) giving ~1.6MB output for a 1-page flyer. Switching to subset=true broke per-char rendering (vectorfeld-dcx per-char loop + pdf-lib subset machinery interacted badly — scattered char output). Need: find the root cause of the interaction, or restructure so per-char draws emit into a single text run via raw TJ ops so subset works. Target: composite.pdf ≤300KB.

title: Hybrid pdfjs-dist text overlay for Type 3 charproc PDFs
description: Per cd2 research (2026-04-20), pdfjs-dist getTextContent() can extract text from Type 3 charproc fonts that MuPDF renders as paths in the SVG writer. Approach: in pdfRender.worker.ts, also call pdfjs-dist getTextContent() in parallel; for any extracted text item whose bbox doesn't intersect a MuPDF <text>, synthesize a <text> from the pdfjs item AND mark/remove overlapping MuPDF <path>s. Deferred until a real Type 3 fixture is in hand — neither user flyer triggers this case (yellow-BG is outlined-at-creation, recoverable by neither engine; noheader has TrueType subsets that MuPDF preserves). When this lands, vectorfeld can edit text in PDFs that today fall back to paths.

title: Remove unused jspdf + svg2pdf.js from production bundle
description: After vectorfeld-9s9 swapped exportPdf to pdf-lib, jspdf and svg2pdf.js are no longer imported by any src/ file. They still ship in the production bundle because they're listed in package.json dependencies. Action: (1) move jspdf to devDependencies (test fixtures still use it via test/roundtrip/helpers/pdfPipeline.test.ts and renderPdf.test.ts), (2) npm uninstall svg2pdf.js entirely, (3) verify bundle shrinks. Non-urgent — just bundle hygiene.

title: Input focus traps keyboard tool shortcuts
description: When a Properties panel input (SW, X, Y, W, H, Rot, etc.) has focus, pressing a tool shortcut key (r, l, p, e, v) types into the input instead of switching tools. The tool shortcut handler in useToolShortcuts checks for INPUT/TEXTAREA focus, but after pressing Enter to commit a value, focus may remain on the input. Found during benchmark automation: after setSW() the next drawRect's 'r' keypress went into the SW field. Workaround: blur active element before pressing tool keys.

title: Pen tool cannot create multi-subpath elements
description: Each pen tool interaction (Enter to commit) creates a separate <path> element. SVG supports multiple subpaths within a single <path> via the m (relative moveto) command, e.g. 'M 10 10 L 20 20 m 50 0 L 80 80'. The pen tool has no way to start a new subpath within the current path. Benchmark paths-data-01 path 1 (X_curve) uses 'm 0 105' to start a second subpath.

title: Canvas SVG overflows flex container on viewport resize
description: When resizing the browser viewport (e.g. to 1280x1080), the canvas SVG element's natural height (from preserveAspectRatio) exceeds its flex container, causing the html element to scroll 403px. The canvas container has overflow:hidden but the parent flex containers don't have min-h-0 to prevent flex children from expanding. Found via playwright-cli benchmark testing.

title: selectTool moves elements twice per frame with smart guides
description: selectTool.ts:563-579 - elements moved to unsnapped position, then smart guides computed, then moved again. Compute snap algebraically first.
close_reason: Closed

title: Smart guides: split candidates by axis, add early exit
description: smartGuides.ts:108-130 - O(edges*candidates) per frame. Pre-split into x/y arrays, break on bestDist===0.
close_reason: Closed

title: TextPath startOffset change bypasses command history
description: PropertiesPanel.tsx:422-425 - direct DOM mutation, not undoable. Use ModifyAttributeCommand.
close_reason: Closed

title: Pencil tool doesn't auto-switch to select after drawing
description: pencilTool.ts:71-103 - all other drawing tools switch to select+set selection. Pencil doesn't.
close_reason: Closed

title: Duplicated unionBBox and transformedAABB wrappers
description: selection.ts and selectTool.ts both have local unionBBox and transformedAABB wrappers. Consolidate into geometry.ts.
close_reason: Closed

title: Pen tool only emits absolute path commands (no relative c/s/m)
description: The pen tool always generates absolute coordinates (M, L, C, Z). It never produces relative commands (m, l, c, s). While visually equivalent, this means exported SVGs differ structurally from hand-authored or imported SVGs that use relative commands. This is cosmetic — relative vs absolute commands render identically — but noted for completeness in benchmark comparison.

title: PropertiesPanel calls detectFillType 3-4 times per render
description: PropertiesPanel.tsx:469-475 - cache result in local variable instead of calling repeatedly.
close_reason: Closed

value: Rule: when fixing or verifying any user-facing change, dogfood through headed-Chromium playwright end-to-end every time. Unit tests alone are insufficient for visual/layout/PDF bugs. Use temp/composite-via-playwright.mjs pattern or extend it. Never claim done without a headed screenshot.
